A_intro: and became a graduate assistant at Cornell University. In 1956 he
A_methods:
returned to the State which he loved and became assistant supervisor
of agricultural education with the Vermont Department of Education.
Bruce served as assistant professor in agricultural education
at Cornell University during 1958 and 1959. His native state again
beckoned and Bruce returned to the University of Vermont in 1960 as
associate professor in agricultural education. He served in this capacity until his medical retirement in 1966.
His professional affiliations and honors included a life membership in the American Association of Teacher Educators in Agriculture,
membership in the American Vocational Association, National Vocational
A_results:
Agricultural Teachers' Association, Vermont Agricultural Teachers’
Association, Vermont Education Association and Phi Delta Kappa. Bruce's
major extracurricular activities were the Alpha Gamma Rho Fraternity and
the Future Farmers of America. He served as State executive secretary
for the FFA and was both a charter member and adviser to his fraternity.
Bruce was a friend to all people with whom he came into contact.
His kindnesses will live in the memories of those who were so furtunate
as to know him.
THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED, that this statement be entered into
the minutes of the American Association of Teachers of Agriculture and
a copy sent to his family.
Gerald R. Buller
Journal of the American Association of University of Vermont
Teacher Educators in Agriculture
Volume 10, Number 1, pp.1
DOI: 10.5032/aatea.1969.01001
